response:
*KEEP THE AREA CLEAN*  
BRUSH GENTLY AND THOROUGHLY TWO TIMES A DAY  
RINSE WITH WATER AFTER EVERY MEAL  
*FLOSS CAREFULLY*  
FLOSS AROUND THE AFFEECTED TOOTH  
BE GENTLE TO AVOID MORE DAMAGE  
*TEMPORARY FILLING MATERIALS*  
USE OVER THE COUNTER MATERIALS  
PROTECT THE TOOTH UNTIL A DENTIST  
*MONITOR FOR CHANGES*  
KEEP AN EYE ON THE TOOTH FOR SIGNS OF INFECTION  
INCREASED PAIN OR SENSITIVITY  
SWELLING OR BAD TASTE IN THE MOUTH  

* * *  

*AVOID HARD, STICKY, OR CHEWEY FOODS*  
STAY AWAY FROM HOT OR COLD FOODS  
PREVENT DAMAGE OR STUCK PARTICLES  
*USE SALIN WATER RINSE*  
RINSE WITH 1/2 TEASPOON SALT IN 8 OUNCES WATER  
REDUCE INFLAMMATION AND BACTERIA  
*SEE DENTAL CARE IMMEDIATELY*  
LOOK FOR DENTAL SERVICES IN YOUR LOCATION  
DENTAL SCHOOLS OR EXPAT COMMUNITIES  
*TRADEITION OF GOOD ORAL HYGIENE*  
MAINTAIN CLEANSE UNTIL A DENTIST  
RELIABLE CARE IS CRUCIAL FOR LONG-TERM HEALTH
